title: A Survival Analysis Guide with Python: Using Time-To-Event Models to Forecast Customer Lifetime
optimized_headline: Unlocking Customer Lifetime: A Guide to Survival Analysis with Python
raw_excerpt:
Understand survival analysis by modeling customer retention through Kaplan-Meier curves and Cox Proportional Hazard regressions.
The post A Survival Analysis Guide with Python: Using Time-To-Event Models to Forecast Customer Lifetime appeared first on Towards Data Science.
raw_body:
Understand survival analysis by modeling customer retention through Kaplan-Meier curves and Cox Proportional Hazard regressions.
The post A Survival Analysis Guide with Python: Using Time-To-Event Models to Forecast Customer Lifetime appeared first on Towards Data Science.
speedrun: A recent guide highlights survival analysis as a method to model customer retention using Python. It emphasizes two key techniques: Kaplan-Meier curves for estimating survival functions and Cox Proportional Hazard regressions for analyzing the impact of variables on retention. This approach allows businesses to predict customer lifetime more accurately. Understanding these models is crucial for companies aiming to improve customer loyalty and retention strategies.
why_it_matters: Businesses can better predict customer behavior, leading to tailored retention efforts and improved customer satisfaction.
This reflects a growing trend in data-driven decision-making, emphasizing the importance of predictive analytics in customer relationship management.

lenses.eli12: Survival analysis helps businesses understand how long customers stay with them. It’s like tracking how long a plant thrives based on sunlight and water. By using this analysis, companies can find ways to keep their customers happy and engaged, which is important for their success.
lenses.pm: For product managers, survival analysis offers insights into user behavior and retention. By understanding customer lifetimes, they can tailor features to enhance engagement. This approach could lead to more efficient marketing strategies and better resource allocation.
lenses.engineer: From a technical perspective, the guide discusses Kaplan-Meier curves and Cox Proportional Hazard models. Kaplan-Meier curves estimate survival rates, while Cox regression assesses how different factors influence retention. These models can provide actionable insights, though they require careful data handling to ensure accuracy.
